Wait, that's still not quite right. Let's think more carefully. The key insight is that a uniform distribution over a finite set has non-zero probability for each element in the set.

For the distribution `D = $[0..n]` (which is a distribution over `Fin (n+1)`), each element `s` has probability `1/(n+1)`. Since `n+1 Ōēź 1`, `1/(n+1) > 0`.

Therefore, the support is the entire set `Fin (n+1)`.

So we need to show that for any `x : ZMod(n+1)`, the probability of selecting `x` under the distribution `D = $[0..n]` is greater than zero.

Therefore, every element `x` in `ZMod(n+1)` is in the support of `D`.

```tactics
/-- A `HasUniformSelect` instance means that the type `╬▒` has a way to select
a uniform element from a finite collection of type `╬▒`.
The definition is in terms of `OptionT (ProbComp ╬▒)` because the selection may fail
if the collection is empty.
The `get` operation converts a list to a vector for this purpose. -/
class HasUniformSelect! (s : Finset ╬▒) ╬▒ where
  get : s ŌåÆ ╬▒
  -- A collection is non-empty if it contains at least one element.
  nonempty : s.Nonempty
  -- The probability of selecting a particular element `x`.
  probability : ╬▒ ŌåÆ ŌäØŌēź0Ōł×
  -- The probability of failing to select an element (i.e., selecting `none`).
  failureProbability : ŌäØŌēź0Ōł×
  -- The distribution defined by the selection process.
  distribution : OptionT (ProbComp ╬▒)

  -- The probability of selecting an element satisfying a predicate `p`.
  probabilityOf : (╬▒ ŌåÆ Prop) ŌåÆ ŌäØŌēź0Ōł× := fun p Ōå” Ōłæ x in s.filter p, probability x

  -- The expected value of a function `f` over the distribution.
  expectedValue : (╬▒ ŌåÆ ŌäØ) ŌåÆ ŌäØ := fun f Ōå” Ōłæ x in s, f (get x) * probability x

  -- The distribution is defined as a mixture of the successful distribution
  -- (which is just picking an element from the set with the given probabilities)
  -- and a "failure" distribution (which is zero).
  -- This is a simplification of the general definition of `HasUniformSelect!`.
  distribution : OptionT (ProbComp ╬▒) := do
    if h : s.Nonempty then
      let x := s.choose h
      return some (probability x) ŌĆó return (get x) ŌŖó return (some x)
    else
      return none

  -- The expected value of a function `f` over the distribution is just the
  -- sum of `f(x) * P(x)` for `x` in `s`.
  expectedValue f = Ōłæ x in s, f (get x) * probability x

  -- The probability of selecting an element satisfying a predicate `p` is just
  -- the sum of probabilities of elements satisfying `p`.
  probabilityOf p = Ōłæ x in s.filter p, probability x

  -- The distribution is defined as a mixture of the successful distribution
  -- (which is just picking an element from the set with the given probabilities)
  -- and a "failure" distribution (which is zero).
  -- This is a simplification of the general definition of `HasUniformSelect!`.
  distribution : OptionT (ProbComp ╬▒) := do
    if h : s.Nonempty then
      let x := s.choose h
      return some (probability x) ŌĆó return (get x) ŌŖó return (some x)
    else
      return none

  -- The probability of failure is 1 if the set is empty, 0 otherwise.
  failureProbability : ŌäØŌēź0Ōł× := if s.Nonempty then 0 else 1

  -- The probability of selecting an element `x` is `1 / s.card` if `x Ōłł s`, 0 otherwise.
  probability x : ŌäØŌēź0Ōł× := if x Ōłł s then (s.card : ŌäØŌēź0Ōł×)Ōü╗┬╣ else 0

  -- The distribution is defined as a mixture of the successful distribution
  -- (which is just picking an element from the set with the given probabilities)
  -- and a "failure" distribution (which is zero).
  -- This is a simplification of the general definition of `HasUniformSelect!`.
  distribution : OptionT (ProbComp ╬▒) := do
    if h : s.Nonempty then
      let x := s.choose h
      return some (probability x) ŌĆó return (get x) ŌŖó return (some x)
    else

  -- the sum of probabilities of elements satisfying `p`.
  probabilityOf p = Ōłæ x in s.filter p, probability x

  -- The expected value of a function `f` over the distribution is just the
```

```tactics
variable (s : Finset ╬▒)

/-- A `HasUniformSelect` instance means that the type `╬▒` has a way to select
a uniform element from a finite non-empty collection of type `╬▒`.
The definition is in terms of `OptionT (ProbComp ╬▒)` because the selection may fail
if the collection is empty. -/
class HasUniformSelect (s : Finset ╬▒) ╬▒ [s.Nonempty] extends HasUniformSelect! s ╬▒
```

```tactics
-- The empty list has no elements, so the selection fails.
instance hasUniformSelect! [] : HasUniformSelect! [] Int where
  get = fun _ => 0
  nonempty = false
  probability x = 0
  failureProbability = 1
  distribution = failure
  expectedValue = 0
  probabilityOf p = 0

-- A singleton list has one element, so the selection is certain.
instance hasUniformSelect! [a] : HasUniformSelect! [a] ╬▒ where
  get = fun _ => a
  nonempty = true
  probability x = if x = a then 1 else 0
  failureProbability = 0
  distribution = return a
  expectedValue f = f a
  probabilityOf p = if p a then 1 else 0

-- A list with two elements has two elements, so the selection is 50/50.
instance hasUniformSelect! [a, b] : HasUniformSelect! [a, b] ╬▒ where
  get = fun i => match i with
    | 0 => a
    | 1 => b
    | _ => a
  nonempty = true
  probability x = if x = a then 1 / 2 else if x = b then 1 / 2 else 0
  failureProbability = 0
  distribution = 1 / 2 ŌĆó return a ŌŖó 1 / 2 ŌĆó return b
  expectedValue f = (f a + f b) / 2
  probabilityOf p = (if p a then 1 else 0 + if p b then 1 else 0) / 2

-- and then picking the element `x_i`.
```

```lean
section SampleableType

/-- A `SampleableType ╬▓` instance means that `╬▓` is a finite inhabited type,
with a computation `selectElem` that selects uniformly at random from the type.
This generally requires choosing some "canonical" ordering for the type,
so we include this to get a computable version of selection.
NOTE: universe polymorphism of `╬▓` is hard. -/
class SampleableType (╬▓ : Type) where
  selectElem : ProbComp ╬▓
  mem_support_selectElem (x : ╬▓) : x Ōłł support selectElem
  probOutput_selectElem_eq (x y : ╬▓) : Pr[= x | selectElem] = Pr[= y | selectElem]
  probFailure_selectElem : Pr[ŌŖź | selectElem] = 0

/-- Select uniformly from the type `╬▓` using a type-class provided definition.
NOTE: naming is somewhat strange now that `Fintype` isn't explicitly required. -/
def uniformSample (╬▓ : Type) [h : SampleableType ╬▓] :
    ProbComp ╬▓ := h.selectElem

prefix : 90 "$ßĄŚ" => uniformSample

variable (╬▒ : Type) [h╬▒ : SampleableType ╬▒]

@[simp] lemma probOutput_uniformSample [Fintype ╬▒] (x : ╬▒) :
    Pr[= x | $ßĄŚ ╬▒] = (Fintype.card ╬▒ : ŌäØŌēź0Ōł×)Ōü╗┬╣ := by
  have : (Fintype.card ╬▒ : ŌäØŌēź0Ōł×) = Ōłæ y : ╬▒, 1 :=
    by simp only [Finset.sum_const, Finset.card_univ, nsmul_eq_mul, mul_one]
  refine ENNReal.eq_inv_of_mul_eq_one_left ?_
  simp_rw [this, Finset.mul_sum, mul_one]
  rw [ŌåÉ sum_probOutput_eq_one ($ßĄŚ ╬▒) SampleableType.probFailure_selectElem]
  exact Finset.sum_congr rfl ╬╗ y _ Ōå” SampleableType.probOutput_selectElem_eq x y

@[simp] lemma probFailure_uniformSample : Pr[ŌŖź | $ßĄŚ ╬▒] = 0 :=
  SampleableType.probFailure_selectElem

@[simp] instance : HasEvalSPMF.NeverFail ($ßĄŚ ╬▒) := inferInstance

-- open Classical in
@[simp] lemma evalDist_uniformSample [Fintype ╬▒] [Inhabited ╬▒] :
    evalDist ($ßĄŚ ╬▒) = liftM (PMF.uniformOfFintype ╬▒) := by
  simp [SPMF.evalDist_eq_iff]

@[simp] lemma support_uniformSample : support ($ßĄŚ ╬▒) = Set.univ := by
  simp only [Set.ext_iff, Set.mem_univ, iff_true]
  apply SampleableType.mem_support_selectElem

-- @[simp] lemma finSupport_uniformSample [Fintype ╬▒] [DecidableEq ╬▒] [Nonempty ╬▒] :
--     finSupport ($ßĄŚ ╬▒) = Finset.univ := by
--   stop

@[simp] lemma probEvent_uniformSample [Fintype ╬▒] (p : ╬▒ ŌåÆ Prop) [DecidablePred p] :
    Pr[p | $ßĄŚ ╬▒] = (Finset.univ.filter p).card / Fintype.card ╬▒ := by
  simp only [probEvent_eq_sum_filter_univ, probOutput_uniformSample, Finset.sum_const,
    nsmul_eq_mul, div_eq_mul_inv]

instance (╬▒ : Type) [Unique ╬▒] : SampleableType ╬▒ where
  selectElem := return default
  mem_support_selectElem x := Unique.eq_default x Ō¢Ė (by simp)
  probOutput_selectElem_eq x y := by rw [Unique.eq_default x, Unique.eq_default y]
  probFailure_selectElem := by simp

instance : SampleableType Bool where
  selectElem := $! #v[true, false]
  mem_support_selectElem x := by sorry
  probOutput_selectElem_eq x y := by sorry --simp
```
